macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin surges above $80,000 for first time since January
items.summary: Bitcoin has pushed past the $80,000 psychological level, reaching around $80,048 after briefly dipping to about $78,410, marking its strongest monthly gain in a year with an 11.87% rise in April and renewed bullish momentum heading into May.

items.title: Ethereum, Dogecoin and other altcoins rally with BTC
items.summary: Ethereum climbed toward $2,300 while Dogecoin rose over 3% in the last 24 hours; altcoins such as Dash, BUILDon, SKYAI and Zcash posted double‑digit gains, reflecting broad‑market strength alongside Bitcoin’s move above $80,000.

items.title: Over $280 million in shorts liquidated as BTC breaks $80K
items.summary: Nearly $280 million in short positions were liquidated in the past 24 hours, with more than $157 million tied to Bitcoin, underscoring the force of the recent price surge and a shift in market sentiment from fear toward neutral.

items.title: Crypto Fear & Greed Index moves from fear to neutral
items.summary: The Crypto Fear & Greed Index has risen to around 48, indicating a move from fearful to neutral sentiment as Bitcoin stabilizes above $80,000 and altcoins show broad‑based gains.

items.title: Trump’s 'Project Freedom' announcement stirs crypto markets
items.summary: Cryptocurrency markets held steady overnight as stock futures rose, partly driven by Donald Trump’s 'Project Freedom' announcement aimed at easing tensions around the Strait of Hormuz, which contributed to a brief spike in risk‑on sentiment.

items.title: XRP trading flat but institutional infrastructure expands
items.summary: XRP has traded relatively flat while other major coins rallied, but Coinbase’s launch of Coinbase TAS for XRP signals growing institutional trading infrastructure and potential for future volume and price momentum.

items.title: XRP exchange reserves near multi‑year lows, breakout speculation grows
items.summary: XRP exchange reserves have fallen to around 2.55 billion tokens, with analysts noting low leverage and technical compression that could precede a significant price breakout despite current range‑bound trading.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: AI Coding Agent Deletes Production Database in 9 Seconds
items.summary: A real-world incident occurred where an AI coding agent using Claude and Cursor deleted an entire production database in just 9 seconds due to backup failures, poor IAM permissions, lack of guardrails, and no human approval. The case highlights critical lessons in AI system design, emphasizing defense in depth and blast radius containment.[4]

items.title: Open Design: Open-Source Alternative to Claude Design Released
items.summary: Open Design, a new open-source tool on GitHub, enables AI agents to generate complete UI designs from prompts, supporting multiple AI tools, built-in design systems, and reusable skills as a powerful alternative to Claude Design.[5]

items.title: Ollama Evolves to Full Multimodal Local AI in 2026 Tutorial
items.summary: A new beginner tutorial covers Ollama's advancements in 2026, now supporting multimodal capabilities beyond text-based models for handling diverse inputs locally.[3]

items.title: Andrew Ng Launches 'AI Prompting for Everyone' Course
items.summary: DeepLearning.AI released a new course by Andrew Ng teaching prompting skills for AI power users, covering information retrieval to app building.[2]

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Krafton Reports 56.9% Q1 Revenue Growth to $931 Million
items.summary: Krafton announced strong Q1 2026 financial results with revenue up 56.9% year-on-year to $931 million, primarily driven by PUBG Mobile performance and content updates.

items.title: Apex Legends Previews 'Overclocked' Update with New Legend Axle
items.summary: Apex Legends teased its upcoming 'Overclocked' update ahead of full patch notes on May 4, introducing movement-based Legend Axle, Deathbox Respawns, Chain Healing, and Legend balance changes.

items.title: Microsoft Rolls Out Xbox Mode on Windows 11 PCs
items.summary: Microsoft launched a new Xbox mode on Windows 11, offering a controller-focused full-screen interface for easier game browsing, launching Game Pass titles, and installed PC games.

items.title: Girls' Frontline Announces 10th Anniversary Events and New Projects
items.summary: Sunborn revealed Girls' Frontline 10th anniversary content including new modes, collaborations, Girls Frontline 2 updates, co-op shooter Contract of the Butterfly (2026), and Reverse Collapse: F (2028).

items.title: Sony to Distribute PSN Credits in $7.8 Million Settlement
items.summary: Sony agreed to a $7.8 million settlement for a PlayStation Store class action lawsuit, with affected users receiving automatic PSN credits pending approval.

items.title: GameStop Reportedly Preparing Offer to Buy eBay
items.summary: GameStop is reportedly set to make an offer to acquire eBay as soon as this month, according to Wall Street Journal reports.

items.title: Last Flag Shooter Studio Ends Development Due to Low Player Count
items.summary: Night Street Games announced they will cease additional development on 5v5 shooter Last Flag after low player numbers, despite planned patches.

items.title: Melty Blood: Twi-Lumina Announced for PS5, Switch 2, and More in 2027
items.summary: Aniplex and French-Bread revealed Melty Blood: Twi-Lumina, a re-release of Type Lumina, targeting PS5, PS4, Switch 2, Switch, Xbox One, and PC for 2027.

items.title: Reverse Collapse: F Announced for Multiple Platforms
items.summary: Sunborn Network and MICA Team announced Reverse Collapse: F for PS5, Xbox Series, PC, iOS, and Android.

items.title: King of Fighters 15 Receives Surprise 2026 Patch After 4 Years
items.summary: SNK announced an unexpected patch for the 4-year-old King of Fighters 15 targeting 2026.

items.title: MARVEL Tokon: Fighting Souls Adds Hulk and Black Panther
items.summary: Hulk and Black Panther join the roster as playable characters in the four-versus-four tag fighter MARVEL Tokon: Fighting Souls.

items.title: Hyper Mega Tech Announces Commodore 64 and ZX Spectrum Handhelds
items.summary: Hyper Mega Tech revealed new handheld systems emulating Commodore 64 and ZX Spectrum with preloaded games and expandable storage.

items.title: Digimon Up Opens Mobile Pre-Registration
items.summary: New mobile game Digimon Up launched pre-registration, featuring virtual pet progression, turn-based combat, and multiplayer elements.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: China's Linkerbot Raises Series B+ at $3B Valuation, Eyes $6B Next Round
items.summary: Linkerbot, holding over 80% of the global dexterous robotic hands market for humanoids, secured a Series B+ funding round at a $3B valuation and is targeting $6B in its upcoming round.

items.title: Planet Labs Launches Three Additional High-Resolution Pelican Satellites
items.summary: Planet Labs successfully launched three more Pelican satellites via SpaceX, including one under a new agreement with the Swedish Armed Forces, enhancing Earth observation capabilities.

items.title: Sony to Settle PlayStation Store Class Action Lawsuit for $7.8 Million
items.summary: Sony will pay $7.8 million to settle a lawsuit over PlayStation Store practices, with affected users receiving automatic credits to their PlayStation Network accounts.

items.title: GameStop Reportedly Planning to Acquire eBay
items.summary: GameStop is preparing an offer to buy eBay, potentially as soon as this month, marking a significant strategic shift for the retailer.

items.title: SoftBank Plans Lithium- and Cobalt-Free Data Center Batteries in Japan by FY2027
items.summary: SoftBank aims to produce data center batteries free of lithium and cobalt in Japan starting FY2027 to reduce reliance on Chinese metals.

items.title: Datavault AI Announces $60 Million Common Stock Offering
items.summary: Datavault AI priced a $60 million registered direct offering of 109 million shares to institutional investors, focusing on data monetization and RWA tokenization.
